# The Sunken Bell of Annun

An old fisherman's tale, retold in every tavern along the Annun coast: a bronze
bell once hung in the harbor watchtower, rung to warn of storms. One winter it
was rung continuously for three days straight with no storm in sight, and the
tower keeper was never seen again. The bell then sank into the harbor itself
without a tower to hold it.

Locals say the bell can still be heard on foggy nights, tolling a warning for
a storm that never comes — or perhaps one that already happened, decades ago,
and the bell is only now catching up.

Fishing crews leave a coin on the harbor wall before sailing, "for the keeper."
